name: fsm-chat
description: Real-time chat between a technician (FSM mobile app) and a dispatcher (FSM shell, side bar), scoped per Service Call / Activity.
//note: This descriptor documents how to register the app inside SAP FSM.
//note2: The SHELL side is a custom Extension placed in the SIDE BAR outlet. The MOBILE side is a Web Container. They are configured in two different places in FSM admin.
//note3: clientIdentifier must match the value sent in the REQUIRE_CONTEXT event by the app (see webapp/Component.js). Register it in the extension's allowed origins / client config.

deployment.//: Replace with your actual hosted URL after you push to GitHub.

extensions.//: DISPATCHER — Shell extension in the SIDE BAR outlet.
extensions.//placement: FSM Shell -> Foundational Services -> Extensions -> Directory -> add custom extension; choose the Side Bar / sidebar outlet as the placement.
extensions.//context: The app initialises the Shell SDK (REQUIRE_CONTEXT) for identity. It also attempts to read the selected Service Call via the Shell SDK; where the platform does not expose it to outlet extensions, the dispatcher enters the Service Call ID in the app's binding panel.
extensions.title: Technician Chat

extensions.//placement: FSM Admin -> (Mobile) Web Containers: create a container pointing at the URL below; surface it in the side menu or a workflow step.
extensions.//context: Mobile web containers pass limited context. For reliable per-activity rooms, pass the Service Call ID explicitly via &objectId=... if your container configuration supports placeholder substitution; otherwise the technician enters the same ID the dispatcher used.
extensions.title: Chat with Dispatcher

parameters.role: technician | dispatcher (sets which side of the chat this instance is)
parameters.objectId: OPTIONAL: Service Call / Activity ID to auto-open that room (skips the manual binding panel)
parameters.userId: OPTIONAL: overrides identity; normally resolved from the Shell SDK
parameters.userName: OPTIONAL: overrides display name; normally resolved from the Shell SDK
parameters.ws: OPTIONAL: WebSocket relay URL for true cross-device real-time, e.g. wss://your-relay
